title: Note de transmission britannique de plusieurs instruments et d'une déclaration britannique concernant les actes signés à Lausanne les 24 juillet et 23 novembre 1923 et certains actes signés à Sèvres le 10 août 1923
text_preview: ・ 酋 • No. 92. (150/31/1924). British Embassy, Paris. August 2nd, 1924. Monsieur le Ministre, In accordance with instructions from His Majesty's Principal Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, I have the honour to transmit to you, herewith, three Instruments conveying His Majesty The King's ratification of (a) the Treaty of Peace with Turkey and such of the other instruments signed at Iausanne on July 24th, 1923, a…

pages.text:
・

酋

•

No. 92.

(150/31/1924).

British Embassy,

Paris.

August 2nd, 1924.

Monsieur le Ministre,

In accordance with instructions from His Majesty's

Principal Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, I have the honour to transmit to you, herewith, three Instruments conveying His Majesty The King's ratification of (a) the Treaty of Peace with Turkey and such of the other instruments signed at Iausanne on July 24th, 1923, as require ratification by

His Majesty; (b) the Greek Minorities Treaty and the Ireaty respecting Thrace, signed at Sèvres on August 10th, 1920, the ratification of which 1s provided for by document No. XVI of the Final Act of the Lausanne Settlement; and (c) the Convention for the assessment and reparation of damage suffered by Allied nationals 1n Turkey, signed at Paris on November 23rd, 1923.

At the same time, I have the honour to enclose

herein the formal declarations regarding the exclusion of certain portions of the British Empire from the Commercial

Convention and from the Convention respecting conditions of

Residence and Business and Jurisdiction. As Your Excellency

18.....

His Excellency,

Monsieur René Renoult,

Ministère des Affaires Etrangères.

..%

Is aware, these declarations have, under Articles 19 and 16 respectively of the two Conventions, to be made at the time of the coming Into force of the Convention. Jour Excellency w111 no doubt arrange for the declarations to be formally made at the time when the proces-verbal of the deposit of ratifications

Is drawn up in accordance with Article 143 of the Peace Ireaty, and the fact that these declarations have been made will doubtless be stated in that proces-verbal.

I am similarly empowered to sign, on behalf of His

Majesty's Government, the Identic declaration excluding the

Condominium of the New Hebrides from the Commercial and

Establishment Conventions as soon as the French Government are in a position to sign on their side.

I have the honour to be, with the highest considera-

tion,

monsieur le kinistre,

Your Excellency's most obedient, :

humble servant,

Eric Phipps
